name: University of Iowa MIS — 24-bit Reference
notes: 24-bit reference recordings, individually miked, split from the University of Iowa Electronic Music Studios instrument library into per-note FLAC files (pp/mf/ff dynamics, vib/novib where performed, rr1/rr2 round-robin where multiple takes exist per note). Loudness: ffmpeg ebur128 integrated loudness (I:) measured across 10 representative mf middle-register zones spanning all 8 instruments (violin, viola, cello, flute, oboe, clarinet, trumpet, horn) -> mean integrated loudness -28.54 LUFS. gainOffsetDb = clamp(-18 - meanLUFS, ±12) = 10.54 dB. Pitch-verified via YIN + FFT narrowband peak-pick + autocorrelation cross-check across all 8 instruments spanning low/mid/high registers: measured fundamentals matched the filename-derived rootMidi within a few cents to ~20 cents in nearly all cases. The extreme top-of-range notes for violin (D6 and above, i.e. the highest handful of notes up to A7) and flute (C7) showed larger, non-fixed deviations (tens to ~200 cents, inconsistent in sign/magnitude) under all three independent pitch-tracking methods -- this does not match the signature of a note-name/octave parsing bug (which would show a constant semitone/octave offset across an entire instrument); it is consistent with genuine weak-fundamental/harmonic-technique recordings at the acoustic limits of those instruments' ranges. Filenames use flat ("b") accidentals only (no sharps present on disk); parsed via standard scientific pitch notation (C4=60, A4=69). DATA-QUALITY FIX: an RMS sweep of the 34 smallest FLAC files on disk found 26 that were near-silent/corrupted splits (true digital silence for most or all of their short duration) rather than valid notes -- these 26 zone entries were REMOVED from this manifest entirely (not just flagged) so the engine cannot select them and play silence; each affected (instrument,dynamic,vib) keyrange group was re-tiled over its surviving zones so no dead/silent keyranges remain (repitch fallback covers the vacated bands from neighboring good samples). Total zones after removal: 1231 (was 1257). Removed files: cello/G3_ff_vib_rr1.flac, oboe/G4_mf_novib_rr1.flac, oboe/Gb4_pp_novib_rr1.flac, viola/A4_mf_vib_rr2.flac, viola/D4_mf_vib_rr1.flac, viola/D6_mf_vib_rr1.flac, viola/D6_mf_vib_rr2.flac, violin/A7_ff_vib_rr1.flac, violin/Ab5_ff_vib_rr2.flac, violin/Ab6_ff_vib_rr1.flac, violin/B6_ff_vib_rr1.flac, violin/B7_ff_vib_rr1.flac, violin/D4_ff_vib_rr2.flac, violin/D7_ff_vib_rr1.flac, violin/Db6_ff_vib_rr2.flac, violin/Db6_mf_vib_rr2.flac, violin/E5_ff_vib_rr1.flac, violin/Eb5_ff_vib_rr2.flac, violin/Eb6_ff_vib_rr1.flac, violin/Eb6_ff_vib_rr2.flac, violin/Eb6_mf_vib_rr2.flac, violin/F6_mf_vib_rr1.flac, violin/F7_mf_vib_rr1.flac, violin/Gb4_ff_vib_rr2.flac, violin/Gb6_ff_vib_rr1.flac, violin/Gb6_mf_vib_rr2.flac. SECOND DATA-QUALITY FIX (post leading/trailing-silence-trim cleanup): after trimming leading/trailing silence across all 1231 zones (onset detection via merged-loud-group analysis), 20 additional zones were found to be silent/near-silent throughout their entire duration with no recoverable attack (peak levels roughly -40 to -80 dBFS across their full length, the same signature as the originally-removed 26) -- these were removed from the manifest and their (instrument,dynamic,vib) keyrange groups re-tiled over the surviving zones using MIDI-midpoint splitting between adjacent root notes (same procedure as the first removal pass). Total zones after this second removal: 1211 (was 1231). Removed files: cello/B4_mf_vib_rr1.flac, cello/D5_mf_vib_rr2.flac, oboe/Eb4_mf_novib_rr1.flac, viola/Eb6_ff_vib_rr1.flac, viola/Gb6_ff_vib_rr1.flac, violin/A5_mf_vib_rr2.flac, violin/B5_mf_vib_rr1.flac, violin/Bb5_ff_vib_rr1.flac, violin/Bb5_ff_vib_rr2.flac, violin/Bb6_mf_vib_rr1.flac, violin/Db5_ff_vib_rr2.flac, violin/Db5_mf_vib_rr2.flac, violin/Db7_mf_vib_rr1.flac, violin/Eb7_mf_vib_rr1.flac, violin/F5_mf_vib_rr2.flac, violin/F6_ff_vib_rr2.flac, violin/F7_ff_vib_rr1.flac, violin/G6_mf_vib_rr1.flac, violin/Gb4_mf_vib_rr2.flac, violin/Gb5_ff_vib_rr2.flac.
